steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Locate the right angle and the angle equal to $W$
steps.content: Check the Pythagorean theorem: $504^2+128^2=270400=520^2$, so the right angle is at $S$ and $TR$ is the hypotenuse. Since $T$ corresponds to $W$, $\tan W=\tan T$.

steps.title: Compute the tangent ratio
steps.content: For angle $T$, the opposite leg is $RS=504$ and the adjacent leg is $ST=128$. $$\tan W=\tan T=\frac{504}{128}=\frac{63}{16}.$$ D is correct.
